details: This scheme "Antim Sanskar Yojana" started by the Labor department of Madhya Pradesh to provide the financial assistance in the case of death. On the death of such an eligible worker, his family members are to be provided financial assistance in the form of funeral expenses.
This scheme aims to provide financial assistance to the dependents of registered construction workers in Madhya Pradesh in the case of their death.
It offers support to alleviate the immediate financial burden on families caused by funeral expenses.
eligibility: Construction worker must be registered with labor department.
Beneficiary must not be received assistance for last rites from any other department.

offline: Applicant need to download the online fill out
select the district Name and choose the option "
Applicant need to submit the application form with required documents
- Photocopy of the first page of the applicant's bank passbook.
- Verification by the management of the institution/establishment at the designated place on the application form.

documents_required: Application form in the prescribed format.

faqs.question: What is the objective of the scheme?
faqs.answer: This scheme "Antim Sanskar Yojana" started by the Labor department of Madhya Pradesh to provide the financial assistance in the case of death.

faqs.question: What are the benefits of the scheme?
How much financial benefits are provided?
faqs.answer: Lump sum financial assistance amount ₹6,000/- will be provided.

faqs.question: How can apply online for the scheme?
faqs.answer: 1. Applicant need to visit the official website 2. Download the online fill out application form. 3. Submit the application form and required documents with concerned department.

faqs.question: What will be online application form?
What is the time period to apply for the benefits of the scheme?
faqs.answer: Beneficiaries need to submit the application with in the 15 days from the death of the construction worker.

faqs.question: What Documents Are Needed ?
faqs.answer: 1 Death certificate of the worker 2. Photocopy of back passbook 3. Verification by the institution/establishment 4. Application form in the prescribed format.

faqs.question: Is this a DBT scheme?
faqs.answer: Yes, this is Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT). Here the money will be directly transferred to the bank account of the beneficiaries.

faqs.question: What is the planning area of this scheme?
May other state person also apply for the scheme?
faqs.answer: No, this scheme is only for Madhya Pradesh state person.
